summary: Adversarial pass on the FORGE-INIT-1 plan (forge init per-file collision check). Plan was reviewed, edited, and approved by the maintainer immediately before this grill; remaining questions would only re-confirm the approved text. No recurring finding classes exist (.factory/history reviews empty). No contradiction with active decisions: edits the canonical harness source, not a vendored copy (0009); one bounded task on one branch (0002); plan-mode honored (0004).
gaps: Plan lacks an explicit '## Surface Impact' section naming user-visible changes
Maintenance coupling: _would_write/GENERATED_FILES must stay in sync with cmd_init's copy loops; drift would silently weaken the collision check
Simpler-shape check: could reuse adopt.py vendor_file instead of a new helper?
resolutions: Surface impact is enumerated inside the plan's Change and Docs sections: the init error message text changes (now lists collisions) and docs/getting-started.md:91 is updated in the same task — nothing user-visible is left implicit

Sync coupling is mitigated in the plan itself: the helper docstring carries the keep-in-sync warning and the collision test pins a COPY_FILES member (WORKFLOW.md), so removing it from the enumeration breaks the test

Reusing adopt's vendor_file was evaluated and rejected in the plan: vendor_file records overwrites after the fact (adopt's clean-tree model), while init needs a pre-flight refusal; extracting a shared helper is a larger diff for no behavior gain — the smaller local helper wins
